citation.article_title: Reservoir computing approaches to recurrent neural network training
citation.author: Lukoševičius
citation.unstructured_citation: Lukoševičius, M., & Jaeger, H. (2009). Reservoir computing approaches to recurrent neural network training. Computer Science Review, 3(3), 127–149. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cosrev.2009.03.005

citation.article_title: Model-free prediction of large spatiotemporally chaotic systems from data: A reservoir computing approach
citation.author: Pathak
citation.unstructured_citation: Pathak, J., Hunt, B., Girvan, M., Lu, Z., & Ott, E. (2018). Model-free prediction of large spatiotemporally chaotic systems from data: A reservoir computing approach. Physical Review Letters, 120(2), 024102. https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.120.024102

citation.journal_title: Differentiable Programming Workshop at Neural Information Processing Systems
citation.unstructured_citation: Kidger, P., & Garcia, C. (2021). Equinox: Neural networks in JAX via callable PyTrees and filtered transformations. Differentiable Programming Workshop at Neural Information Processing Systems.

citation.author: Kidger
citation.unstructured_citation: Kidger, P. (2021). On neural differential equations [PhD thesis]. University of Oxford.

citation.article_title: Reconstruction, forecasting, and stability of chaotic dynamics from partial data
citation.author: Özalp
citation.journal_title: Chaos: An Interdisciplinary Journal of Nonlinear Science

citation.journal_title: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering

citation.article_title: ReservoirPy: An efficient and user-friendly library to design echo state networks
citation.author: Trouvain
citation.journal_title: Artificial neural networks and machine learning – ICANN 2020
citation.unstructured_citation: Trouvain, N., Pedrelli, L., Dinh, T. T., & Hinaut, X. (2020). ReservoirPy: An efficient and user-friendly library to design echo state networks. Artificial Neural Networks and Machine Learning – ICANN 2020, 494–505. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-61616-8_40

citation.article_title: Common task framework for a critical evaluation of scientific machine learning algorithms
citation.author: Wyder
citation.journal_title: Neural information processing systems 2025 datasets and benchmarks track
citation.unstructured_citation: Wyder, P. M., Goldfeder, J. A., Yermakov, A., Zhao, Y., Riva, S., Williams, J. P., Zoro, D., Rude, A. S., Tomasetto, M., Germany, J., Bakarji, J., Maierhofer, G., Cranmer, M., & Kutz, J. N. (2025). Common task framework for a critical evaluation of scientific machine learning algorithms. Neural Information Processing Systems 2025 Datasets and Benchmarks Track. https://openreview.net/forum?id=pkFKjmUE3L
